lede: A look at December 7 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Torri Huske and Alessandro Marchi, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Lando Norris
events.desc: British racing driver Lando Norris wins the Formula One World Drivers' Championship in 2025 with McLaren.

events.title: JAXA
events.desc: The JAXA probe Akatsuki successfully enters orbit around Venus five years after the first attempt.

events.title: Furry convention
events.desc: The annual furry convention Midwest FurFest is targeted in an unsolved chlorine gas attack.

events.title: Galileo (spacecraft)
events.desc: The Galileo spacecraft arrives at Jupiter, a little more than six years after it was launched by Space Shuttle Atlantis during Mission STS-34.

events.title: Iberia (airline)
events.desc: An Iberia Airlines Boeing 727 collides with an Aviaco DC-9 in dense fog while the two airliners are taxiing down the runway at Madrid–Barajas Airport, killing 93 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Attack on Pearl Harbor: The Imperial Japanese Navy carries out a surprise attack on the United States Pacific Fleet and its defending Army and Marine air forces at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii. (For Japan's near-simultaneous attacks on Eastern Hemisphere targets, see December 8.)

events.title: Cricket
events.desc: Australian cricketer Jack Fingleton becomes the first player to score centuries in four consecutive Test innings.

events.title: Physicist
events.desc: German-born Swiss physicist Albert Einstein is granted an American visa.

events.title: Justin II
events.desc: Byzantine Emperor Justin II, suffering recurring seizures of insanity, adopts his general Tiberius and proclaims him as Caesar.

events.title: Cicero
events.desc: Marcus Tullius Cicero is assassinated in Formia on orders of Marcus Antonius.

births.name: Peter Laviolette
births.line: American ice hockey player and coach

deaths.text: John C. England, United States Navy officer
Cicero, Roman philosopher, lawyer, and politician (born 106 BC)

observances.text: Eve of the Immaculate Conception-related observances: Day of the Little Candles, begins after sunset (Colombia)
